Defines macros for C/C++ compatibility and string manipulation.

# Purpose
This C header file provides macro definitions to facilitate compatibility between C and C++ code. It uses the `#pragma once` directive to prevent multiple inclusions of the file. The macros `__BEGIN_DECLS` and `__END_DECLS` are defined to ensure that C++ compilers treat the enclosed declarations as C code, using `extern "C"` linkage when compiled with a C++ compiler. The `__P` macro is defined to simplify function prototype declarations, while `__CONCAT` and `__STRING` macros are used to concatenate tokens and convert tokens to string literals, respectively. These macros help in writing portable and interoperable code across C and C++ environments.
